contact.restrictions: Applicants must be a registered 501(c)(3) organizations, and the Foundation will give preference to those organizations providing services that align with the goals and communities served by Northwest Community Bank. The Foundation does not provide financial support to political organizations or candidates, activities for religious purposes, individual endeavors, or organizations which are discriminatory in their practices. Additionally, the Foundation will not fund proposals for the following: organizations which utilize a fiscal agent; alumni or endowment campaigns; fundraising events; sports teams; advertising; lobbying; travel; and/or other private foundations. Organizations may apply once per grant cycle, and annual grants will not exceed $25,000 per recipient.
contact.how_to_apply: Please visit the website for complete instructions on how to apply for a grant: https://nwcommunitybank.com/foundation/ Potential applicants will be required to create an account with the Foundation's Online Portal in order to submit their application materials. Applicants may be asked to detail the program for which they are requesting funding, and to explain how their proposals fit within the Foundation's core focus areas, which include: education; housing; human services; and community impact. Applicants may be asked to illustrate the impact and budget of their proposals, and may also be asked to provide proof of their qualified 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status, including IRS Letters and previously filed tax returns.

contact.restrictions: ORGANIZATIONS SHOULD BE BETWEEN TWO AND FIFTEEN YEARS OLD, AND PROVIDE COUNSELING, ROLE MODELING, MENTORING OR SIMILAR PROGRAMS. THEY CANNOT SUBMIT MORE THAN ONE REQUEST PER 12-MONTH PERIOD IF AWARDED DURING THAT PERIOD. ORGANIZATIONS ARE CAPPED AT $25,000 IN LIFETIME FUNDING OR FIVE FUNDED GRANT APPLICATIONS.
contact.how_to_apply: APPLICATIONS SHOULD BE SUBMITTED IN WRITING AND SHOULD INCLUDE: 1)YOUR ORGANIZATION'S NAME, AGE, MISSION STATEMENT, AND WEB ADDRESS (IF APPLICABLE); 2) A STATEMENT INDICATING WHAT FUNDING YOU ARE REQUESTING AND HOW THE MONIES WOULD BE USED IF GRANTED; 3) THE NAMES AND TITLES OF YOUR BOARD MEMBERS; 4) THE NAME OF THE CONTACT PERSON FOR DIRECTING QUESTIONS; 5) A CLEAR COPY OF YOUR IRS FORM 501(C)3; 6) A COPY OF YOUR MOST RECENT BUDGET.

contact.restrictions: Lumina carefully reviews every grant inquiry that meets foundation guidelines. Following the initial review, we will inform applicants within two months of receipt whether the inquiry fits our current priorities and resources. Due to limited resources, we cannot request proposals from all inquiries that meet our criteria. If we decide to request a full proposal, a Program Officer will contact the applicant to request additional information and to provide instructions on applying for a grant. An invitation to submit a proposal does not guarantee funding. Lumina carefully considers all solicited proposals; occasionally, we invite external experts to review proposals as well. We are diligent in our efforts to respond promptly to proposals. Assuming potential grantees' timely responses to requests for information, the entire review and approval process typically takes approximately six months.
contact.how_to_apply: Lumina Foundation does not have a formal letter of inquiry (LOI) policy. However, we do review unsolicited requests. Unsolicited requests are reviewed by the grants management team for alignment with the Foundation's current strategic priorities. If the request aligns with current work, it is referred to a program officer for further review. Requests considered viable are invited to submit concept papers for further consideration by Lumina Executive Team members. Formal invitations for full proposal may be advanced following initial evaluation.
